sections.title: Cell Structure and Biomolecules
sections.description: Build a strong Class 11 Biology foundation with cell theory, cell organelles, and the major biomolecules of life.

lessons.title: The Cell: Basic Unit of Life
lessons.description: Understand cell theory, prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, and the role of cell organelles.

lessons.title: Cell Organelles and Their Functions
lessons.description: Study nucleus, mitochondria, ribosomes, Golgi apparatus, endoplasmic reticulum, lysosomes, and plastids.

lessons.title: Biomolecules Overview
lessons.description: Learn the structure and importance of carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, and nucleic acids.

topics.title: Cell Theory
topics.description: Principles of cell theory and why cells are considered the structural and functional unit of life.

topics.title: Membrane-bound Organelles
topics.description: Functions of organelles and how compartmentalization improves efficiency in eukaryotic cells.

topics.title: Macromolecules of Life
topics.description: Properties and biological roles of biomolecules in cellular structure and metabolism.

examples.title: Comparing Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic Cells
examples.description: A quick comparison table that helps students identify major structural differences.
examples.code:
Prokaryotic cell: no true nucleus, smaller ribosomes, no membrane-bound organelles.
Eukaryotic cell: true nucleus, membrane-bound organelles present, larger and more complex.

examples.title: Why Mitochondria Are Called the Powerhouse
examples.description: Connect ATP production with the role of mitochondria in respiration.
examples.code: Mitochondria perform aerobic respiration and generate ATP, which powers most cellular activities.

questions.question: Which cell organelle is primarily responsible for ATP production?

sections.title: Plant Physiology: Transport and Photosynthesis
sections.description: Cover the core processes of water transport, mineral nutrition, and photosynthesis in Class 11 plant physiology.

lessons.title: Transport in Plants
lessons.description: Learn about diffusion, osmosis, imbibition, xylem transport, and translocation in phloem.

lessons.title: Mineral Nutrition in Plants
lessons.description: Understand essential nutrients, deficiency symptoms, and nutrient cycles in plants.

lessons.title: Photosynthesis in Higher Plants
lessons.description: Study pigments, light reactions, Calvin cycle, and factors affecting photosynthesis.

topics.title: Osmosis and Water Potential
topics.description: Movement of water across membranes and its role in plant cells.

topics.title: Ascent of Sap
topics.description: Cohesion-tension theory and the role of transpiration pull.

topics.title: Photosynthetic Pigments and Light Reaction
topics.description: How chlorophyll and accessory pigments capture light to produce ATP and NADPH.

examples.title: Transpiration Pull Example
examples.description: A simple explanation of how water travels from roots to leaves.
examples.code: When water evaporates from leaf surfaces, a pull is created in xylem vessels. Cohesion between water molecules keeps the water column intact.

examples.title: Photosynthesis Equation
examples.description: Use the balanced equation to connect inputs and outputs of photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which tissue is responsible for the transport of water in plants?
The Calvin cycle takes place in which part of the chloroplast?

sections.title: Human Physiology: Digestion, Respiration and Circulation
sections.description: Introduce the major Class 11 human physiology systems with digestive processes, breathing mechanics, and blood circulation.

lessons.title: Digestion and Absorption
lessons.description: Understand the human digestive tract, digestive glands, enzymes, and nutrient absorption.

lessons.title: Breathing and Exchange of Gases
lessons.description: Learn breathing mechanism, respiratory volumes, and transport of gases in blood.

lessons.title: Body Fluids and Circulation
lessons.description: Study blood composition, heart structure, cardiac cycle, and blood vessels.

topics.title: Digestive Enzymes
topics.description: How enzymes like amylase, pepsin, trypsin, and lipase help break down food.

topics.title: Oxygen Dissociation and Gas Transport
topics.description: Role of hemoglobin and the transport of oxygen and carbon dioxide.

topics.title: Cardiac Cycle
topics.description: Sequence of events in one heartbeat including systole and diastole.

examples.title: Path of Food Through the Alimentary Canal
examples.description: A flow-based example to help students visualize the digestive process.
examples.code: Mouth -> Buccal cavity -> Esophagus -> Stomach -> Small intestine -> Large intestine -> Rectum -> Anus

examples.title: Double Circulation
examples.description: Why humans have separate pulmonary and systemic circulation.
examples.code:
Pulmonary circulation: heart -> lungs -> heart
Systemic circulation: heart -> body -> heart

questions.question: Which component of blood is primarily responsible for oxygen transport?
Which part of the digestive system is the main site of nutrient absorption?
